parameters.jsCode:
const data = $input.first().json;
const { topic, difficulty, duration_min, duration_sec, num_clips, word_target } = data;
const { core_theory, hook_line, narrative_angle, visual_suggestions, scientific_depth, target_audience, category } = data;
const topic_research = data.topic_research || '';

const sceneList = Array.from({ length: num_clips }, (_, i) => {
  const start = i * 5, end = Math.min(start + 5, duration_sec), dur = end - start;
  const mm = s => Math.floor(s/60) + ':' + String(s%60).padStart(2,'0');
  return `Scene ${i+1} (${mm(start)} - ${mm(end)}, ${dur}s, ~${Math.round(dur * 2.5)} words)`;
}).join('\n  ');

const prompt = `You are a world-class physics documentary writer. Your job is to CREATE everything from scratch Ã¢â‚¬â€ hook, narration, scenes, title, hashtags. The Google Sheet context below is REFERENCE ONLY to help you understand the topic deeply. Do NOT copy or reuse the hook_idea or angle verbatim Ã¢â‚¬â€ use them only as inspiration.

--- REFERENCE CONTEXT (for understanding only, do not copy) ---
Topic: ${topic}
Category: ${category || ''}
Core Theory: ${core_theory}
Scientific Depth: ${scientific_depth}
Hook Idea (reference only): ${hook_line}
Narrative Angle (reference only): ${narrative_angle}
Visual Style Reference: ${visual_suggestions}
Target Audience: ${target_audience || 'Advanced physics enthusiasts'}

--- RESEARCH CONTEXT ---
${topic_research}

--- YOUR TASK ---
Create a complete, original ${duration_sec}-second physics video script that does THREE things:
1. HOOKS the viewer instantly with a specific, topic-direct opening sentence
2. EXPLAINS the topic clearly through storytelling Ã¢â‚¬â€ not a lecture, not vague wonder
3. RESOLVES the curiosity Ã¢â‚¬â€ by second ${duration_sec}, viewer must UNDERSTAND what this phenomenon is and why it matters

Ã¢Å¡Â Ã¯Â¸Â CRITICAL: You MUST return EXACTLY ${num_clips} scenes. Each is 5 seconds. Non-negotiable.

NARRATION Ã¢â‚¬â€ THE COMPLETE RULES:

RULE 1 Ã¢â‚¬â€ HOOK (Scene 1 first sentence):
- Must name or directly demonstrate the TOPIC in the first 5 words
- Must be a specific physical fact, not a question, not an observation about life
- Drops the viewer INTO the physics Ã¢â‚¬â€ no runway, no setup, no context-setting
- BAD: "The universe is stranger than we think"
- BAD: "What if I told you particles behave oddly"
- GOOD: "Quantum tunneling lets particles pass through walls they have no energy to cross"
- GOOD: "A neutron star packs the mass of the sun into a city-sized sphere"

RULE 2 Ã¢â‚¬â€ CLARITY THROUGH STORY (Middle Scenes):
- Every sentence must make the topic MORE understood, not just more mysterious
- Tell it like a chain of events: first this happens, then that happens, here is why
- Use concrete physical descriptions Ã¢â‚¬â€ what you would literally SEE or MEASURE
- Each scene answers one question the previous scene raised
- NO vague language: no "somehow", "in a sense", "in a way", "kind of", "sort of"
- NO abstract filler: no "this changes everything", "reality is not what it seems", "the universe is strange"
- NO personal pronouns: NEVER use "I", "we", "you", "our", "your", "let us", "join us" Ã¢â‚¬â€ pure documentary narrator voice, speak about the physics not to the viewer
- Short punchy sentences. Rhythm matters Ã¢â‚¬â€ must sound powerful spoken aloud

RULE 3 Ã¢â‚¬â€ RESOLUTION (Scene ${num_clips}):
- The final sentence must DELIVER understanding AND wonder together
- Viewer must feel: "Now I get it Ã¢â‚¬â€ and it's incredible"
- NOT just: "The universe is mysterious" (empty)
- YES: "Every computer chip on Earth works because electrons borrow energy they do not have" (understanding + impact)

RULE 4 Ã¢â‚¬â€ WORD BUDGET:
- Total: TARGET 28-36 words. Minimum 24 words, absolute maximum 40 words. This becomes ONE short on-screen caption â€” it must read as a single tight paragraph, not a wall of text.
- ~${Math.round(word_target/num_clips)} words per scene
- The ${num_clips} scenes flow as ONE continuous story Ã¢â‚¬â€ no repetition, no filler

SCENE STRUCTURE (EXACTLY ${num_clips} scenes, 5 seconds each):
  ${sceneList}
- Scene 1: HOOK Ã¢â‚¬â€ specific physical fact directly about ${topic}. First word counts.
- Middle Scenes: DEEPEN Ã¢â‚¬â€ what exactly is happening? Describe the physical reality concretely.
- Scene ${num_clips}: RESOLUTION Ã¢â‚¬â€ delivers understanding + wonder. Viewer walks away knowing something real.

â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•
VISUAL DIRECTOR BRIEF â€” READ BEFORE WRITING ANY VISUAL PROMPT
â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•

You are a VISUAL DIRECTOR creating cinematic quantum physics simulations. Target quality: Blender Cycles / Houdini / Unreal Engine 5 â€” photorealistic, dramatic, scientifically accurate. Think CERN collision renders, PBS Space Time animations, high-end science documentary VFX.

MOST IMPORTANT RULE: Every topic must look COMPLETELY DIFFERENT visually. Quantum tunneling looks nothing like black hole evaporation. Wave-particle duality looks nothing like nuclear fusion. The visual content must reflect the SPECIFIC PHYSICS of THAT topic â€” never reuse generic visuals across topics.

â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€
STEP 1: VISUAL THINKING â€” DO THIS FOR EACH SCENE BEFORE WRITING
â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€
Ask 3 questions per scene before writing the prompt:
  Q1: What is PHYSICALLY HAPPENING in the narration right now? (literally, not metaphorically)
  Q2: At what SCALE does this happen? (sub-atomic / atomic / molecular / macro / cosmic)
  Q3: What would you ACTUALLY SEE through a perfect physics camera at that exact scale?
Answer Q3 â†’ that is your visual subject. Then dress it in the quality standard below.

EXAMPLE â€” narration: "An electron borrows energy to tunnel through a barrier it cannot classically cross"
  Q1: Electron wavefunction encounters potential energy barrier, quantum-mechanically tunnels through
  Q2: Sub-atomic, angstrom scale
  Q3: Diffuse glowing electron probability cloud hits sharp translucent energy barrier wall, wavefunction decays exponentially inside wall, bright blue point re-emerges on far side
  Result: "Slow tracking shot following electric-blue electron probability cloud drifting toward a razor-sharp translucent amber energy barrier wall in deep black void, cloud compresses and dims exponentially inside barrier, small bright blue point re-emerging from far side, volumetric blue god-rays, subsurface glow on cloud edges, cinematic depth of field, Blender Cycles quality, 720x1280 portrait"

â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€
STEP 2: 4-CLIP NARRATIVE ARC â€” MANDATORY VISUAL PROGRESSION
â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€
The 4 clips are 4 documentary shots that travel through the concept:
  CLIP 1 â€” ESTABLISH: Full subject at natural scale. Set the scene. Where are we, what are we looking at?
  CLIP 2 â€” DIVE DEEPER: Internal structure, components, forces at work. What is inside or underneath?
  CLIP 3 â€” THE MOMENT: The critical physics EVENT that defines this topic. Peak behavior. The unique thing.
  CLIP 4 â€” CONSEQUENCE: The result, aftermath, wider implication, or scale revelation. Resolution.

Each clip MUST be VISUALLY DISTINCT â€” different element, different scale, different moment. The viewer must feel they are TRAVELLING through the concept, not watching the same object from 4 angles.

â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€
STEP 3: PHYSICS VISUAL VOCABULARY â€” CHOOSE WHAT FITS YOUR TOPIC
â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€
QUANTUM SCALE:
- Electron probability cloud: transparent diffuse glowing lobe, electric blue or violet, shifts from blurred superposition to sharp collapsed point on measurement
- Wave function interference: two luminous waves overlapping, bright constructive nodes, dark destructive antinodes, colored fringe pattern propagating forward
- Quantum tunneling: wavefunction hitting glowing translucent energy barrier, decaying exponentially inside barrier material, re-emerging on far side
- Quantum entanglement: two spheres (electric blue + amber orange) connected by braided helical shimmering beam, synchronized pulsing, correlated color flashes
- Superposition: single particle showing two simultaneous ghost trajectories both glowing, collapsing to one on observation
- Zero-point vacuum: brief flashing virtual particle-antiparticle pairs blinking into and out of existence in void
- Spin: particle with quantum spin visualized as clockwise or counterclockwise luminous spiral wrapping around it

ATOMIC SCALE:
- Nucleus: dense compact sphere, warm amber-gold subsurface scattering, intense white-hot core, proton-neutron sub-spheres packed inside, energy haze radiating outward
- Electron shells: concentric luminous shells at discrete energy radii, electrons as bright moving points, quantum jumps shown as flashes with photon emission
- Photon emission: electron dropping orbital level, single photon wave-packet propagating outward as expanding ripple of light
- Radioactive decay: unstable wobbling nucleus, alpha or beta particle ejecting with recoil, daughter nucleus settling to stability
- Atomic bonding: charge-density energy bridge glowing between two atom spheres, bond brightness shows bond strength

PARTICLE PHYSICS:
- Collision: two particle beams converging, collision produces radial burst of colored debris tracks curving in magnetic field like bubble chamber trails, slow-motion shockwave ring expanding outward
- Quark structure: inside a proton â€” three color-charged spheres (red, green, blue quarks) connected by glowing gluon flux tube strings, tubes stretching and snapping to create new quark pairs
- Antimatter annihilation: matter particle (warm gold) + antimatter (electric blue) spiraling together, contact = pure white-hot flash expanding as gamma ray burst
- Force carriers: virtual particles as brief faint connecting flashes between interacting particles (photons/gluons/W-Z bosons)
- Higgs field: space filled with shimmering transparent field, particles moving through gaining mass shown as resistance drag-glow around them

FIELD THEORY:
- Electric field: point charge at center, curved luminous field lines radiating outward in all directions, line density shows field strength, test charge following field line path
- Magnetic dipole: luminous field lines looping pole-to-pole through 3D space, iron-filings pattern made of light
- EM wave: E-field (electric blue ribbon) and B-field (amber ribbon) oscillating perpendicular to each other and to propagation direction, wave visibly moving forward
- Casimir vacuum: two parallel plates in void, virtual particles flashing between them, plates slowly drawn together by quantum force

COSMOLOGICAL:
- Black hole: perfect dark sphere, orange-white accretion disk spiraling inward with Doppler color shift, extreme gravitational lensing bending star field around equator, polar plasma jets
- Spacetime curvature: flat perspective grid warped into deep gravitational well funnel by massive object, test particles rolling along curved geodesic paths
- Gravitational waves: two massive bodies orbiting, spacetime fabric rippling outward in concentric ring waves like surface of disturbed water
- Cosmic inflation: singularity point of infinite brightness expanding outward as fireball, matter and energy condensing from the expanding front

THERMODYNAMIC / STATISTICAL:
- Phase transition: ordered glowing crystal lattice dissolving into chaotic fast-moving particle swarm
- Bose-Einstein condensate: hot fast-moving particles (warm orange) cooling until wave functions overlap and merge into single coherent cold-blue ground state
- Entropy: ordered particle arrangement spontaneously randomizing, energy dispersing from concentrated to diffuse

â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€
STEP 4: COLOR â€” MAP TO PHYSICAL MEANING, NOT DECORATION
â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€
Color must mean something physically. Every color choice must justify itself:
- QUANTUM BLUE (electric cyan, ice blue, deep cobalt): cold quantum states, wave functions, superposition, entanglement, coherence
- PLASMA ORANGE/GOLD (amber, white-hot core, ember red): nuclear energy, thermal kinetics, particle cores, hot dense matter
- EM PURPLE/MAGENTA (violet, electric pink): electromagnetic fields, force carriers, radiation, photon phenomena
- RESONANCE TEAL (cyan-teal, lime-green): wave interference, coherent states, resonance, constructive patterns
- GRAVITY (deep blue-black with silver-white distortion): spacetime curvature, dark matter, gravitational lensing
- WHITE-HOT: single peak energy point only â€” collision center, collapse, annihilation flash â€” never as a background fill
- BLUE plus ORANGE PAIRING: signature contrast for dual states, entangled pairs, matter vs antimatter, wave vs particle
Each of the 4 clips should have its own dominant color building a visual arc. Same glow quality and saturation level across all 4.

â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€
STEP 5: CAMERA â€” CHOOSE WHAT BEST SERVES THIS SPECIFIC PHYSICS
â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€
- SLOW MACRO PUSH-IN: builds tension, pulls viewer into quantum scale, subject grows to fill frame â€” use for establishing small-scale subjects
- ORBITAL ARC (30-60 degree sweep): exposes 3D structure and geometry from multiple angles â€” use for showing full geometry of a phenomenon
- STATIC LOCK: freezes on exact critical physics moment â€” collision, collapse, snap, annihilation â€” use when the event itself is the focus
- SLOW PULL-BACK: reveals context and scale, the subject we were close to is part of something much larger â€” use for consequence scenes
- TRACKING SHOT: follows a particle, wave front, or photon through space â€” use for propagation, tunneling, beam travel
- RAPID SCALE ZOOM OUT: shows atomic subject is inside a macroscopic system â€” use for scale-contrast reveals
- ROTATING ORBIT: circles a symmetric 3D structure continuously â€” use for atoms, black holes, dipole fields
All moves: smooth, fluid, continuous within the 5-second clip. Depth of field always on â€” sharp on subject, soft bokeh on background.

â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€
STEP 6: UNBREAKABLE QUALITY STANDARDS â€” IDENTICAL ACROSS EVERY VIDEO FOREVER
â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€
ENVIRONMENT (always exactly one of these):
- Deep pure black void: subject suspended in darkness, completely isolated, nothing competing with it
- Deep space: faint distant star field, subtle nebula haze, infinite depth behind subject
- Spacetime grid: faint perspective grid receding to horizon (for gravity and field theory topics)
- Lab environment: ultra-clean blue-lit scientific chamber, chrome and glass instruments, neon accent edges (for experiment topics)

LIGHTING (all light comes FROM the physics, never from outside):
- The physics subject IS the primary light source â€” it illuminates the void around it
- Contrasting rim light wraps subject edge (blue subject = orange rim; orange subject = blue rim)
- Volumetric god-rays fan outward from bright energy cores into surrounding darkness
- Bloom and glow on all luminous elements, glow radius proportional to energy intensity
- Lens flare only at single peak energy moment per video, never decorative
- Subtle floating micro-particle dust in void for atmospheric depth
- Zero flat lighting. Zero overhead uniform illumination. Zero studio lighting appearance.

ANIMATION (everything in motion â€” no frozen frames ever):
- Every element shows motion: rotation, propagation, pulsing, streaming, spiraling, or flowing
- Physics events follow arc within each clip: build tension, peak moment, aftermath
- Particles never hover static â€” always in trajectory or field-constrained motion
- Fields breathe and pulse with steady rhythm throughout the clip
- Energy connections such as beams, bonds, and flux tubes continuously flow and pulse along their length

RENDER QUALITY:
- Photorealistic 3D CGI: Blender Cycles / Houdini / Unreal Engine 5 level
- Subsurface scattering on all sphere and particle subjects
- Volumetric light scattering in beams, halos, and god-rays
- Ray-traced reflections on glass and metallic surfaces
- Anti-aliased smooth energy trails, zero pixelation on light streaks
- Subject fills 60-80% of 720x1280 portrait frame, centered, never cropped at edges
- Cinematic depth of field throughout every clip

STRICTLY FORBIDDEN IN EVERY CLIP:
- Text, labels, numbers, letters, symbols, watermarks of any kind
- Human faces or body parts
- Cartoon, anime, cel-shading, or flat illustrated style
- Static frozen scene with no visible motion
- Cluttered backgrounds competing with the physics subject
- Flat or uniform studio lighting
- The same visual subject repeated across multiple clips â€” each clip must be visually distinct
- Generic glowing orb used as substitute for the actual specific physics phenomenon named in the narration

Return ONLY a raw JSON object Ã¢â‚¬â€ no markdown, no explanation:
{
  "narration": "full narration, all scenes joined as one continuous speech",
  "scenes": [
    {
      "scene_number": 1,
      "start_sec": 0,
      "end_sec": 5,
      "narration_segment": "exact words for this scene",
      "visual_prompt": "[CAMERA MOVE from foundation â€” clip 1=push-in, clip 2=orbital arc, clip 3=lock/zoom, clip 4=pull-back], [EXACT PHYSICS SUBJECT from narration â€” name it specifically, never generic], [MOTION BEHAVIOR â€” how it moves, pulses, flows, spins], [DOMINANT COLOR from foundation palette â€” blue/orange/purple/teal, state which and why], [LIGHTING â€” subject as light source, contrasting rim light, volumetric god-rays, bloom], [ATMOSPHERE â€” deep black void or space background, subtle particle dust depth], photorealistic 3D CGI Blender Cycles quality, subsurface scattering on particle spheres, volumetric glow, cinematic depth of field, 720x1280 portrait",
      "negative_prompt": "text, letters, numbers, labels, watermarks, UI, human faces, cartoon, anime, blurry, low quality, flat lighting"
    }
  ],
  "title": "punchy title under 60 characters",
  "hashtags": "Generate 15-20 hashtags. Mix: 5 niche topic-specific tags (#quantumtunneling #wavefunction), 5 broad physics tags (#physics #quantumphysics #astrophysics #science #cosmology), 5 viral/discovery tags (#mindblown #spacefacts #didyouknow #learnscience #sciencefacts), 3-5 platform tags (#shorts #reels #scienceshorts). All lowercase, # prefix, space-separated.", "social_caption": "Write a detailed social media post caption in EXACTLY 3 paragraphs (250-350 words total). This is NOT the video narration. This is the full description posted on YouTube, TikTok, Instagram.  PARAGRAPH 1 - THE HOOK & CORE FACT (3-4 sentences): Open with the single most surprising or counter-intuitive fact about this topic. Include a specific number, measurement, or comparison to make it concrete. Then state what this phenomenon actually IS in plain language.  PARAGRAPH 2 - THE EXPLANATION (4-5 sentences): Explain the mechanism step by step. What causes it? What happens physically? Use real scientific terms but explain each one briefly. Connect it to something the reader already knows to build understanding. Give the key insight that makes it click.  PARAGRAPH 3 - WHY IT MATTERS (3-4 sentences): What does this reveal about the universe, physics, or science? What problem does it solve or what mystery does it deepen? End with an open question or implication that makes readers want to research further.  STRICT RULES: No personal pronouns ever (no I, we, you, our, your). No vague phrases: no somehow, in a sense, in a way, the universe is strange. Every sentence must add specific information, never repeat or pad. Pure documentary tone throughout. DO NOT include hashtags in this field - hashtags go in the separate hashtags field.",
  "highlight_keywords": ["MainWord1", "MainWord2", "MainWord3"]
}`;

return [{ json: { ...data, claude_script_request: {
  model: 'claude-sonnet-4-6',
  max_tokens: 8192,
  messages: [{ role: 'user', content: prompt }]
}}}];

const data = $input.first().json;
const payload = {
  social_caption: data.social_caption || '',
  narration: data.narration,
  clip_prompts: data.clip_prompts,
  audio_path: data.audio_path,
  subs_path: data.subs_path,
  audio_duration: data.audio_duration,
  subtitle_chunks: data.subtitle_chunks,
  word_timings: data.word_timings,
  bgm_path: data.bgm_path || null,
  negative_prompts: data.negative_prompts || [],
  highlight_keywords: data.highlight_keywords || [],
  scenes: data.scenes || [],
  sheet_row: data.sheet_row
};
return [{ json: {
  job_id: data.job_id,
  topic: data.topic,
  title: data.title,
  hashtags: data.hashtags,
  social_caption: data.social_caption || '',
  sheet_row: data.sheet_row,
  payload_json: JSON.stringify(payload),
  readyForVideoAt: new Date().toISOString()
} }];

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ model: 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001', max_tokens: 1024, messages: [{ role: 'user', content: 'You are a physics expert. Research this topic for a physics YouTube video: ' + $json.topic + '. Answer: 1) What does this phenomenon LOOK LIKE visually? (colors, shapes, movement, scale, glow, sparks, waves etc) 2) What is the key experiment setup? (equipment, lab setup, what is visible) 3) What are the 3 most visually striking moments? 4) What measurable quantities are visible? (wavelengths, voltages, angles, speeds) 5) Best camera angle to show this? Be specific, scientific, visual. 200-300 words, plain text.' }] }) }}
